purpose: Single authorized Clarabel dependency-remediation discovery: locate a Clarabel Python distribution whose INSTALLED Settings object exposes BOTH static_regularization_eps AND static_regularization_proportional.

original_invalid_clarabel.reason_invalid: required field static_regularization_eps ABSENT from the installed Settings object

remediation_search.method: API-CAPABILITY discovery only — field existence on the INSTALLED Settings object. NOT dependency shopping: no corpus was solved, no residual, speed or economic result influenced this search.
remediation_search.finding: NO Clarabel Python release exposes `static_regularization_eps`. Across the entire probed release history (0.6.0 -> 0.11.1) the base static-regularization control is uniformly named `static_regularization_constant`, and `static_regularization_proportional` exists SEPARATELY in every release.
remediation_search.conclusion: The authorized remediation — find a build exposing static_regularization_eps — CANNOT SUCCEED. No such Python distribution exists.

base_control.note: the installed default is ALREADY exactly the 1e-8 requested for pinning

capability_assessment.why_not: The rejected substitution was using `static_regularization_proportional` IN PLACE OF `static_regularization_eps`, collapsing two distinct controls into one. That is NOT the situation: BOTH controls exist as separate fields and would be pinned separately. The divergence is a Python-BINDING NAME for the base control, stable across every release. `static_regularization_eps` is the Rust/Julia field name.

disposition: AWAITING OWNER RULING — the authorized remediation is provably unavailable.

options_for_owner.action: Register `static_regularization_constant = 1e-8` as the base static-regularization control, pinned separately from `static_regularization_proportional`.
options_for_owner.effect: Clarabel becomes VALID; the independent cross-verifier is retained.
options_for_owner.risk: None identified: both controls are pinned separately and read back; no control is omitted, aliased or collapsed.

options_for_owner.action: Declare Clarabel permanently INVALID for this adjudication; proceed HiGHS-only under the registered selection rule (§9).
options_for_owner.effect: No independent Clarabel verifier qualifies. HiGHS must solve all 3,895 alone.
options_for_owner.risk: Loss of the algorithmically independent cross-check.

recommendation: A — the owner's stated concern (two distinct controls must not be conflated) is fully satisfied; the obstacle is a binding-level field name, not a missing capability.
